memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Eaton, Eton, Eaten and others. First found in Cheshire where they were seated from very ancient times, some say well before the Norman Conquest and the arrival of Duke William at Hastings in 1066 C.E. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Alexander Eaton who settled in Boston Mass. in 1651; Eliza Eaton settled in Pennsylvania in 1682; Francis Eaton, his wife Sarah, and son Samuel, arrived on the "Mayflower" and settled in Plymouth, Mass. in 1620. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Motto Translated: Truth conquers all things. Recorded in many surname spellings including Ayton, Eaton, Eton, Eyton, Iton, Iteen and apparently the extraordinary Eighteen, this interesting name is usually of Old English and Anglo-Saxon pre-7th century origins. It is a locational surname deriving from any one of the numerous places called Eaton, Eton, and Ayton, found in several counties of England. Most of these places are named from the Old English words "ea", meaning river, and "tun", a farm or settlement. They are variously recorded in the famous Domesday Book of 1086 in the spellings of Etone, Etune, Ettuna, and Ettone. It is therefore easy to see how the later surname spellings developed over the centuries. However there is also a probability that at least some of the later surname spellings developed from the French name "Etienne" or "Estienne", forms of the personal name Stephen, and well recorded amongst the Huguenot protestant refugees of the 17th century. Examples of London recordings which may help to show the surname development include the christening of John Eaton, at St. Lawrence Jewry, on 20 January 1566, and the marriage of Edward Eton and Clemence Jordan at St. Mary Woolchurch, on 15 October 1570. Blais Etienne was recorded at the French Church, Threadneedle Street, on 6 January 1656, Charlotte Eteen at St Pancras Old Church, on 24 February 1844, and Henry Eighteen, at St Pauls, Deptford, on 7 December 1851. The first recorded spelling of the family name is believed to be that of Peter de Eton. This was dated 1273, in the "Hundred Rolls" of the county of Huntingdonshire", during the reign of King Edward 1st of England, 1272 - 1307.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 – 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Cicely de Eton於1311年出生在英國爾斯托普特時，她的父親Nicholas de Eton23歲，母親Joan de Stockport20歲。她於大約1341年與Sir Edward de Warenne在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1361年在她的家鄉享年50歲。

zh-hans.Poynton with Worth, Cheshire, England: 英国爾波易頓 煙雨 沃斯
zh-hans.Macclesfield, Cheshire, England: 英国爾馬可萊斯費爾德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Cicely de Eton于1311年出生在英国爾斯托普特时，她的父亲Nicholas de Eton23岁，母亲Joan de Stockport20岁。她于大约1341年与Sir Edward de Warenne在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1361年在她的家乡享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

variants.name: Eaton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Eaton or Eton, such as Eaton Socon (Bedfordshire), Eaton (Cheshire), or Eton (Buckinghamshire), named from either Old English ēa ‘river’ or ēg ‘island, low-lying land’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.
History: Nathaneal Eaton, born in Coventry, England, c. 1609, came to MA in 1637 and was the first head of Harvard College, in 1638–39.

nameMeanings.name: Cicely
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Cecily .  This was a common form of the name in the Middle Ages, and in the 20th century was well known as the name of the British actress and singer  Cicely Courtneidge  (  1893–1980 ).

variants.name: Cecily
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Cicely de Eton was born in 1311, in Stockport, Cheshire, England, her father, Nicholas de Eton, was 23 and her mother, Joan de Stockport, was 20. She married Sir Edward de Warenne about 1341, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1361, in her hometown, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Cicely d’Eton est née en 1311, à Stockport, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas d’Eton, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Joan de Stockport, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Edward de Warenne environ 1341, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1361, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Cicely de Eton 1311 in Stockport, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nicholas de Eton 23 und ihre Mutter Joan de Stockport 20. Sie hat um 1341 in England Sir Edward de Warenne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb 1361 im Alter von 50 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Cicely de Eton è nata il nel 1311 nel Stockport, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Nicholas de Eton, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Joan de Stockport, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Sir Edward de Warenne circa 1341, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1361, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Cicely de Etonが1311年にイギリスチェシュアイアストクプオルトで生まれた時，父Nicholas de Etonは23歳，母Joan de Stockportは20歳であった。約1341年にイギリスでSir Edward de Warenneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1361年に故郷で50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Cicely de Eton(이)가 1311년 영국 체실르 스톡포트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas de Eton(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Stockport(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1341년 영국에서 Sir Edward de Warenne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1361년 그녀의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Cicely de Eton nasceu em 1311, em Stockport, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nicholas de Eton, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Stockport, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Edward de Warenne aproximadamente 1341, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1361, em sua cidade natal, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Cicely de Eton родилась в 1311 г. в местности стоккпорт, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas de Eton было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Joan de Stockport, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Edward de Warenne около 1341 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1361 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Cicely de Eton nació en 1311, en Stockport, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas de Eton, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Joan de Stockport, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Sir Edward de Warenne aproximadamente 1341, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1361, en su pueblo, a la edad de 50 años.
